page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The lack of integrated GPUs and local NVMe storage represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this server specifically for memory-bound workloads rather than graphics processing or high-IOPS local caching.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r5.16xlarge offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for enterprise applications that require large memory footprints without the overhead of GPU hardware. Appropriate workloads include in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this server provides 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications, such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics, where massive RAM capacity is prioritized over local disk I/O or graphics acceleration.
